assessor_raw:
## Baseline Assessment — eval_scenario_332, Run 1

The 'reporting intensity' network feature counts distinct recipients who flag a message via the security toolbar within 24 hours post-delivery. The label definition in Section 1 explicitly includes user-reported phishing as one of the phishing=1 sources. This creates a target leakage path: messages that accrue high reporting intensity are disproportionately labeled phishing=1 because those same reports may trigger the analyst investigation that confirms the label. More importantly, this feature is entirely unavailable at real-time scoring time — no recipients have reported anything when the message first arrives. Training with reporting intensity in the feature set produces evaluation results that cannot be replicated in deployment, because the feature will always be 0 or absent at inference time. This is a material evaluation invalidity. Verdict: **critique_wins**
